name: Cefazolin
executiveSummary: First-generation IV cephalosporin; surgical prophylaxis and MSSA.
indications:
## Indications

- Surgical prophylaxis of skin and soft tissues.
- Infections caused by MSSA and susceptible streptococcus.

## Precautions

- Adjust in CKD. Beta-lactam allergy.

> Adjust per institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Vial with lyophilized powder containing 1 g (Cefazolin Drawer, Fabra, Klonal, Northia, Richet, Pharmavial).
adulto.reconstitution: 10 mL of DW or NS. Conc: 100 mg/mL.
adulto.diluent: 1 g in 100 mL of NS or D5W.
adulto.administration: IM: Yes. Reconstitute with 4 mL of DW and administer deep IM. Direct IV: Yes. Reconstitute with 10 mL of DW or NS and administer over 3–5 min. Intermittent IV: Yes. Dilute in 50–100 mL of NS or D5W and administer over 30–60 min.
adulto.notes: Compatible with NS and D5W. May cause phlebitis via direct IV route. IM administration is possible. First choice for surgical prophylaxis.

stability:
## Reconstituted (in the vial)

- 24 h at room temperature, 10 days refrigerated.

## Diluted solution (to be administered)

- 24 h at room temperature, 10 days refrigerated.

bibliography.citation: Servicio de Infectología, Prevención y Control de Infecciones. UCIP 2026 — Guía de dilución y estabilidad.
ANMAT. Información de medicamentos y prospectos autorizados en Argentina.
Sociedad Argentina de Infectología (SADI). Guías y consensos.
